name: 埃里克·萨蒂
brief: 萨蒂出生于法国翁弗勒尔，是音乐史上最古怪的先锋派人物之一。他在巴黎音乐学院被认为毫无才华，后来在蒙马特的酒吧「黑猫」担任钢琴师。他是玫瑰十字教团的短暂成员，创立过只有自己一人的「领导与统治耶稣艺术之都主教座堂教会」。他的生活极度简朴——去世后人们在他凌乱的小公寓里发现了数百封未拆的信件。40岁时他重返音乐学院以学生身份学习对位法，令人震惊。
briefEn: Born in Honfleur, France, Satie was one of the most eccentric avant-garde figures in music history. Dismissed as talentless at the Paris Conservatoire, he went on to play piano at Le Chat Noir cabaret in Montmartre. He briefly joined the Rosicrucian order and founded a church of which he was the sole member. His life was one of extreme austerity — after his death, hundreds of unopened letters were discovered in his cluttered studio apartment. At the age of forty, he astonished everyone by re-enrolling at the Schola Cantorum as a student to study counterpoint.
contribution: 萨蒂是20世纪实验音乐的鼻祖。他的「家具音乐」（Musique d'ameublement）概念——设计为不被注意的背景音乐——直接预示了布莱恩·伊诺的环境音乐（Ambient Music）。他用反讽和幽默挑战了音乐的严肃传统，在乐谱上写满了荒诞的演奏指示。他的极简风格比简约主义运动早了半个多世纪。
contributionEn: Satie was the godfather of twentieth-century experimental music. His concept of "furniture music" (Musique d'ameublement) — sound designed to blend into the background unnoticed — directly foreshadowed Brian Eno's ambient music. He wielded irony and humor as weapons against the solemn traditions of concert music, filling his scores with absurd performance directions. His stripped-down aesthetic anticipated the Minimalist movement by more than half a century.
style: 萨蒂的音乐以极度简约、静态和反传统著称。他拒绝浪漫主义的情感洪流，追求一种冷静、透明、近乎空白的美学。他的和声使用未解决的七和弦和九和弦的平行进行，创造出一种悬浮不定的氛围。他的音乐没有高潮、没有发展、没有起承转合——只有纯粹的声音存在于时间之中。他也是最早使用重复作为结构原则的作曲家之一。
styleEn: Satie's music is defined by radical simplicity, stasis, and defiance of convention. Rejecting the emotional torrent of Romanticism, he pursued a cool, transparent, almost blank aesthetic. His harmony relies on parallel progressions of unresolved seventh and ninth chords, producing a sensation of weightless suspension. There are no climaxes, no development, no dramatic arcs — only pure sound existing in time. He was also among the earliest composers to employ repetition as a structural principle.

famousScores.name: 《吉姆诺佩迪》三首
famousScores.desc: 缓慢、静谧、忧伤的钢琴小品，当代流行文化中最常被引用的古典音乐之一
famousScores.descEn: Slow, serene, and melancholy piano pieces — among the most frequently quoted classical works in contemporary popular culture
famousScores.detail:
《吉姆诺佩迪》三首（1888）是萨蒂最广为人知的作品，也是他音乐美学的最纯粹体现之一。「Gymnopédie」是萨蒂自造的词，可能来源于古希腊的裸体舞蹈仪式，也可能只是他为了神秘感而编造的。三首乐曲的结构极为简洁：稳定的四三拍低音伴奏（模仿古希腊陶罐上的舞者），加上飘忽不定的旋律线条，以降D大调和G大调的和弦漫游创造出一种无处可归的漂泊感。

这三首作品在萨蒂生前几乎无人知晓。他的挚友德彪西于1896年将其中两首（第一和第三首）改编为管弦乐，才使其引起注意。萨蒂对德彪西的这次「援救」心存感激，但也有些矛盾——他既希望被人理解，又担心被人太容易理解。这种两难困境贯穿了他的一生。

今天，《吉姆诺佩迪》几乎无处不在——它出现在无数电影、广告、餐厅背景音乐和钢琴学习者的练习中。它的广泛流传既证明了萨蒂的创见，又似乎印证了他对「家具音乐」的预言——这首曲子真的被无数场合用作「不被注意的背景」。这其中的反讽意味，想必萨蒂地下有知，会微微一笑。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Trois Gymnopédies (1888) are Satie's best-known works and among the purest expressions of his musical aesthetic. "Gymnopédie" is a word of Satie's own coinage, possibly derived from ancient Greek naked-dance rituals, or possibly invented simply for its aura of mystery. Each of the three pieces has an extremely spare structure: a steady 3/4 bass accompaniment (evoking the dancers on ancient Greek vases) beneath a meandering melodic line, with harmonic wandering between D-flat major and G major chords creating a sensation of homeless drifting.

These three pieces were almost entirely unknown during Satie's lifetime. His close friend Debussy orchestrated two of them (the first and third) in 1896, bringing them to wider attention. Satie was grateful for Debussy's act of rescue but also conflicted — he wanted to be understood while fearing being understood too easily. This dilemma pursued him throughout his life.

Today, the Gymnopédies are omnipresent — in films, advertisements, restaurant background music, and piano student practice sessions alike. Their ubiquity confirms Satie's originality while also vindicating his prediction about "furniture music" — this piece has literally become, in countless contexts, "unnoticed background." Satie, wherever he is, would probably allow himself a small ironic smile.

famousScores.name: 《格诺西安舞曲》三首
famousScores.desc: 无拍号、无小节线，自由而神秘的冥想音乐
famousScores.descEn: Written without time signatures or bar lines — free-flowing, enigmatic meditations
famousScores.detail:
《格诺西安舞曲》（1890）三首（加上后来的另外三首，共六首）是萨蒂在实验精神上更进一步的作品。「Gnossienne」同样是萨蒂自造的词，可能与克里特岛的克诺索斯宫殿或诺斯替教派（Gnosticism）有关。最显著的创新是：乐谱中完全没有拍号、没有小节线，旋律在不受节拍结构束缚的情况下自由流动——这在1890年代是颠覆性的写法。

萨蒂在这套作品的乐谱中留下了一些极为奇特的演奏指示，这也是他荒诞风格的早期体现。例如：「以深刻的虔诚感」（Avec une profonde piété）、「带着沉重的感」（Avec beaucoup de maladie）、「更加仔细地」（Avec étonnement）……这些指示既非完全无意义，又无法被字面理解，介于诗意和玩笑之间的灰色地带，如同萨蒂本人的个性。

《格诺西安舞曲》的和声语言与《吉姆诺佩迪》相近，但更加神秘、更加东方化——弗里吉亚调式和多利亚调式的运用，以及与中东音乐相通的旋律形态，使其充满了一种来自另一个文明的遥远回响。三首作品至今是钢琴文献中最迷人的小品之一，在世界各地被广泛演奏和引用。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Trois Gnossiennes (1890) — the first three of an eventual six — represent a further step in Satie's experimental journey. "Gnossienne" is another word of his own invention, possibly relating to the Palace of Knossos on Crete or to Gnosticism. The most radical innovation is the complete absence of time signatures and barlines from the score — the melody flows freely, unconstrained by metrical structure, which was deeply subversive in the 1890s.

Satie left some extraordinarily strange performance markings in these scores — an early manifestation of his absurdist style: "With deep piety" (Avec une profonde piété), "With a great sense of sickness" (Avec beaucoup de maladie), "With astonishment" (Avec étonnement)… These directions are neither entirely meaningless nor capable of literal interpretation, inhabiting a gray zone between poetry and joke — just like Satie's own personality.

The harmonic language of the Gnossiennes resembles the Gymnopédies but is more mysterious and more Eastern in flavor — the use of Phrygian and Dorian modes, and melodic contours that resonate with Middle Eastern music, creates the sense of a distant echo from another civilization. All three pieces remain among the most captivating miniatures in the piano literature, widely performed and quoted around the world.

famousScores.name: 《游行》 Parade
famousScores.desc: 与毕加索、科克托合作的芭蕾舞剧，使用了打字机和汽笛
famousScores.descEn: A ballet created in collaboration with Picasso and Cocteau, incorporating typewriters and sirens into the score
famousScores.detail:
《游行》（1917）是萨蒂与剧作家科克托（Jean Cocteau）和画家毕加索（Pablo Picasso）合作的芭蕾舞剧，由迪亚基列夫的俄罗斯芭蕾舞团首演。这是20世纪最重要的跨艺术合作之一：毕加索设计了包括立体主义风格服装和布景，科克托提供了剧本，萨蒂的音乐则使用了打字机、手枪声、汽笛、飞机螺旋桨和彩票摇号机等「噪音」乐器——在1917年，这是彻头彻尾的疯狂之举。

1917年5月18日在巴黎首演，观众席爆发了激烈的骚动——据目击者描述，有人大声叫喊「德国佬！」（指萨蒂等人是文化叛徒），还有人用手杖威胁演员。批评家基约姆·阿波利奈尔（Guillaume Apollinaire）的节目单文章中首次使用了「超现实主义」（Surréalisme）一词——这个词后来成就了整个艺术运动。萨蒂因为写信嘲讽一位恶评他的批评家，被判处八天监禁（后经朋友帮助豁免）。

《游行》在艺术史上意义非凡：它是达达主义和超现实主义精神在音乐中的最早体现，也是「现成品」（ready-made）概念在音乐上的应用——将日常生活的声音直接引入音乐厅，挑战了艺术与现实的边界。科克托后来将这次合作总结为「让马戏团蒙娜丽莎微笑」。
famousScores.detailEn:
Parade (1917) is a ballet created in collaboration with playwright Jean Cocteau and painter Pablo Picasso, premiered by Diaghilev's Ballets Russes. It is one of the most important cross-disciplinary collaborations of the twentieth century: Picasso designed the Cubist costumes and set, Cocteau provided the scenario, and Satie's score incorporated "noise" instruments including a typewriter, gunshots, sirens, an airplane propeller, and a lottery wheel — in 1917, this was utterly scandalous.

At the Paris premiere on May 18, 1917, the audience erupted in fierce uproar — eyewitnesses reported shouts of "Boches!" (accusing Satie and his collaborators of cultural treason) and canes brandished at performers. The program notes by Guillaume Apollinaire used for the first time the word "Surréalisme" — a word that would go on to name an entire art movement. Satie, for writing a mocking letter to a hostile critic, was sentenced to eight days in prison (later commuted thanks to friends' intervention).

Parade is of enormous significance in art history: it is among the earliest embodiments in music of the Dadaist and Surrealist spirit, and an application to music of the "ready-made" concept — importing the sounds of everyday life directly into the concert hall and challenging the boundary between art and reality. Cocteau later summarized the collaboration as "making the circus smile like the Mona Lisa."

famousScores.name: 《烦恼》 Vexations
famousScores.desc: 一页主题重复840次，约18小时，极端重复的先驱
famousScores.descEn: A single page of music repeated 840 times over roughly eighteen hours — a landmark of extreme repetition
famousScores.detail:
《烦恼》（约1893年，确切日期不明）是一页只有80个音符的钢琴作品，但萨蒂在乐谱顶端写下了这样一句话：「为了把这个主题演奏840遍，在非常缓慢的动作下，有必要在开始之前通过一种非常深沉的宁静来做好自己的准备。」如果严格遵照此指示演奏，演出时长约需18小时。

这部作品在萨蒂生前从未公开演出，甚至不确定他是否认真对待过它。直到1963年，约翰·凯奇组织了首次完整演出——在纽约，由18名钢琴家轮流演奏，历时18小时40分钟。演出结束时，据说凯奇大声喊道：「谢谢你，萨蒂先生！」当晚留到最后的听众没有几人，但这次演出成为极简主义和观念艺术史上最具传奇色彩的事件之一。

《烦恼》是萨蒂对「音乐究竟是什么」的最激进追问——840次重复是正念冥想还是惩罚式折磨？它是艺术还是玩笑？是天才还是恶作剧？这种多义性本身，正是萨蒂作为思想家的最大魅力所在。凯奇视《烦恼》为「一切重复音乐的起点」，史蒂夫·赖希和菲利普·格拉斯也承认其精神上的先驱地位。
famousScores.detailEn:
Vexations (c. 1893; exact date uncertain) is a page of eighty notes for piano, but above the score Satie wrote: "In order to play this theme 840 times in succession, it would be as well to prepare oneself beforehand, in the deepest silence, by serious immobility." Performed strictly according to this instruction, the total duration is approximately eighteen hours.

The piece was never performed publicly during Satie's lifetime, and it is uncertain whether he ever took it entirely seriously. It was only in 1963 that John Cage organized the first complete performance — in New York, with eighteen pianists taking turns over eighteen hours and forty minutes. At the end, Cage is said to have cried out "Thank you, Monsieur Satie!" Few listeners remained to the end, but the event became one of the most legendary occasions in the history of Minimalism and conceptual art.

Vexations is Satie's most radical interrogation of what music is: are 840 repetitions mindful meditation or punitive ordeal? Is this art or a joke? Genius or prank? This very ambiguity is the greatest charm of Satie as a thinker. Cage regarded Vexations as "the starting point for all repetition music"; Steve Reich and Philip Glass have both acknowledged its spiritual primacy.

famousScores.name: 《干燥的胚胎》 Embryons desséchés
famousScores.desc: 带有荒诞演奏指示的钢琴组曲
famousScores.descEn: A piano suite peppered with absurd performance instructions
famousScores.detail:
《干燥的胚胎》（1913）是萨蒂荒诞幽默风格的极致体现，三首钢琴小品分别以「钩虾的胚胎」「独眼巨虾的胚胎」和「长腿虫的胚胎」命名，乐谱上写满了荒唐的演奏指示和旁白注释，如「像夜莺一样，牙疼」「遥远而柔软得像蛋」「用手心演奏」，以及对其他作曲家的戏谑引用。

其中最著名的是对肖邦《葬礼进行曲》的引用——萨蒂在引用时加注：「著名的马祖卡：作者：舒伯特」（Sic），故意张冠李戴。这种对「高雅传统」的调侃，在当时令许多保守派音乐人感到冒犯，却令德彪西和科克托等先锋人士捧腹大笑。萨蒂在乐谱封面上还特别注明：「演奏者请不要在表演时大声读出文字。」（但这恰恰是在邀请人们这样做。）

《干燥的胚胎》是萨蒂「讽刺系列」作品中最具代表性的一首，也是音乐幽默史上最成功的作品之一。它以一种完全不同于「传统音乐笑话」的方式制造幽默——不是在音乐结构上开玩笑，而是通过文字与音乐之间的荒诞错位，创造出一种后现代式的、无休止的自我解构。
famousScores.detailEn:
Embryons desséchés (1913) represents the full flowering of Satie's absurdist humor. Three piano pieces bearing the names "Embryo of a Shrimp," "Embryo of a Holothuria," and "Embryo of a Pod Shrimp" are strewn throughout with outlandish performance directions and narrative asides: "like a nightingale with a toothache," "distant and soft as an egg," "play with the palms of the hands" — as well as mocking quotations from other composers.

The most celebrated passage is a quotation of Chopin's Funeral March, annotated by Satie: "Famous Mazurka by: Schubert" (Sic) — a deliberate attribution to the wrong composer. This mockery of the "great tradition" offended conservative musicians of the day while reducing Debussy, Cocteau, and other avant-garde figures to helpless laughter. The score's cover bears the additional instruction: "Please do not read the text aloud during the performance." (Which is, of course, a perfect invitation to do exactly that.)

Embryons desséchés is the most representative work of Satie's "ironic series" and one of the most successful comic works in music history. It generates humor in a completely different mode from "traditional musical jokes" — not by playing games with musical structure but through the absurd disjunction between text and music, creating a postmodern, endlessly self-deconstructing comedy.

famousScores.name: 《苏格拉底》（交响戏剧）
famousScores.desc: 萨蒂最严肃的作品，以柏拉图对话录为文本，平静如水却震撼人心
famousScores.descEn: Satie's most serious work — a setting of Plato's dialogues, calm as still water yet profoundly moving
famousScores.detail:
《苏格拉底》（1919）是萨蒂作品中最令人震惊的，因为它与人们通常对他的印象截然不同：既无讽刺，也无幽默，只有一种纯净、近乎空无的音乐，托起柏拉图对话录（《菲德洛斯》、《席美亚斯》）中关于苏格拉底生死的文字。女高音独唱（或独白者）以近乎没有情感起伏的平静声调朗唱苏格拉底饮鸩而死的场景，管弦乐则以不带任何情绪色彩的稀薄织体为其作底——在最戏剧性的时刻，音乐反而最克制。

作品首演时，听众完全不知如何反应——这不像他们熟悉的任何东西。有人说是乏味，有人说是伟大。而萨蒂本人则说：「我写这部作品时，内心充满了爱。」这是他极罕见的一次表达真挚情感。德彪西、科克托和拉威尔听后，都承认这是他最重要的作品。

《苏格拉底》被约翰·凯奇视为自己最重要的精神来源之一——凯奇的「无意图的音乐」理念与《苏格拉底》的「无情感的音乐」遥相呼应。这部作品以其对音乐「纯粹性」的极端追求，成为20世纪音乐史上最具争议也最有深度的作品之一，每隔数十年就会被重新「发现」和重新解读。
famousScores.detailEn:
Socrate (1919) is the most startling of Satie's works, because it is entirely unlike what people expect of him: no irony, no humor — only a pure, near-empty music carrying the text of Plato's dialogues (the Phaedrus and the Phaedo) describing the life and death of Socrates. A soprano soloist (or speaker) intones the scene of Socrates drinking hemlock in a voice of almost completely flat affect; the orchestra provides a thin, emotionally colorless accompaniment — and at the most dramatic moments, the music becomes most restrained.

At its premiere, audiences had no idea how to react — it resembled nothing they knew. Some called it tedious; some called it great. Satie himself said: "I wrote this work with my heart full of love." It was one of his extremely rare expressions of sincere feeling. Debussy, Cocteau, and Ravel all agreed, on hearing it, that it was his most important work.

John Cage regarded Socrate as one of his most important spiritual sources — Cage's concept of "purposeless music" resonates profoundly with Socrate's "emotionless music." Through its extreme pursuit of musical "purity," this work is one of the most controversial and deeply significant in twentieth-century music history, rediscovered and reinterpreted anew every few decades.

famousScores.name: 《体育与娱乐》
famousScores.desc: 二十幅文字与音乐的双联画，法国先锋艺术的珍品
famousScores.descEn: Twenty text-music diptychs — a precious gem of the French avant-garde
famousScores.detail:
《体育与娱乐》（1914）是萨蒂与艺术家夏尔·马丹（Charles Martin）合作的一本限量版豪华艺术书籍，每一页左边是马丹的水彩插画，右边是萨蒂短小的钢琴曲和文字，共21首（其中一首是关于「乔拉尔先生的打嗝」的幽默散文）。主题包括高尔夫、花卉市场、荡秋千、打猎、钓鱼……每首只有几小节，却各有其精妙的形态。

萨蒂在创作前言中写道：「这本书有两种形式：有趣的和无聊的。我建议您选择有趣的。」这句典型的萨蒂式格言，也是对整部作品的最好注脚——他以一贯的调侃态度对待严肃的艺术生产，但他的音乐本身从来不是开玩笑。其中《华尔兹》（La Valse）一首，仅用6个和弦在3小节内描绘了一个旋转的舞会，微型而完整。

《体育与娱乐》在当时的限量版原著印了100本，每本标价约为两台大型管弦乐队的演出费用——萨蒂嘲讽说这个价格「象征性地」与内容相符。这部作品是音乐与视觉艺术跨媒体合作的早期典范，也是萨蒂将荒诞与精确完美融合的代表性作品。
famousScores.detailEn:
Sports et Divertissements (1914) is a luxury limited-edition artist's book produced in collaboration with the illustrator Charles Martin. Each page pairs one of Martin's watercolors with a brief Satie piano piece and text — twenty-one in total (including one humorous prose piece about "Monsieur Golse's belching"). The subjects include golf, a flower market, swings, hunting, fishing… each piece lasts only a few bars yet has its own perfect form.

Satie's preface to the collection states: "This book has two forms: one amusing, one tiresome. I advise you to choose the amusing one." This characteristically Satian epigram is the best gloss on the whole project — he approached serious artistic production with his habitual irony, but the music itself was never a joke. The Waltz piece, for instance, depicts a spinning ballroom in just six chords over three bars — miniature and complete.

The original limited edition was printed in one hundred copies, each priced at roughly the equivalent of two full orchestra concert fees — Satie remarked that this price "symbolically" matched the content. Sports et Divertissements is an early model of cross-media collaboration between music and visual art, and one of the most representative examples of Satie's perfect fusion of absurdity and precision.

famousScores.name: 《杰克在盒子里》
famousScores.desc: 遗失数十年后被发现，完整的萨蒂式幽默小交响曲
famousScores.descEn: Lost for decades, then rediscovered — a perfect miniature of Satian wit
famousScores.detail:
《杰克在盒子里》（约1899年作，管弦乐配器由迪亚基列夫完成于1926年）有着一段传奇的失而复得的历史。萨蒂将这部钢琴小品的乐谱遗忘在一辆出租车上，以为从此再无音讯，几十年后却奇迹般地被找到。萨蒂死后，俄罗斯芭蕾舞团将其改编为芭蕾，在1926年首演——萨蒂再也无缘见到这次迟到的首演。

这部三乐章的小品保留了萨蒂一贯的特质：轻快的外衣下藏着奇异的和声转向，每当听者以为抓住了乐曲的走向，它便以一个出人意料的和弦滑走，如同那个永远在最不可预料时刻弹出的弹簧玩具。其中对卡巴莱舞蹈和音乐厅流行歌曲的引用，透露出萨蒂在蒙马特「黑猫」酒吧弹琴生涯对他音乐语言的深刻印记。

《杰克在盒子里》常被演奏者作为音乐会的开场小品，它的简短和幽默使其在严肃的音乐会程序中成为一个受欢迎的「空气清新剂」。更重要的是，它证明了萨蒂即使在写所谓的「轻松」音乐时，也保持着高度的技艺和个人风格，是理解他整体创作面貌的一个必要侧面。
famousScores.detailEn:
Jack in the Box (composed around 1899; orchestrated by Milhaud for the Ballets Russes in 1926) has a legendary history of loss and recovery. Satie left the piano score in a taxi and assumed it gone forever; decades later it was miraculously found. He had died before the Ballets Russes staged it as a ballet in 1926 — he never lived to see this belated premiere.

The three-movement miniature preserves Satie's characteristic quality: a light, breezy exterior concealing surprising harmonic turns — whenever the listener thinks they have grasped the music's direction, an unexpected chord causes it to slip away, like the spring-loaded toy that always leaps out at the most unpredictable moment. Quotations from cabaret dance and music-hall popular song betray how deeply his years playing piano at Le Chat Noir in Montmartre had marked his musical language.

Jack in the Box is frequently used as a concert opener; its brevity and wit make it a welcome "breath of fresh air" in a serious program. More significantly, it demonstrates that even when writing supposedly "light" music, Satie maintained high craft and a distinctive personal voice — an essential dimension for understanding the full range of his creative output.

famousScores.name: 《我爱你》（华尔兹）Je te veux
famousScores.desc: 萨蒂为女歌手而写的最受欢迎的歌曲，优雅的蒙马特风情
famousScores.descEn: Satie's most popular song, written for a female singer — elegant Montmartre atmosphere
famousScores.detail:
《我爱你》（约1900年）是萨蒂为他倾心的歌手波琳·德·格雷西（Paulette Darty）写的一首舞厅华尔兹，也是他所有作品中最具大众吸引力的一首。那时的萨蒂还在蒙马特的各大酒吧和音乐厅谋生，接触了大量法国轻音乐和香颂的传统，这首歌完美地继承了这一传统的优雅与感伤。

歌曲以简单但充满魅力的华尔兹节奏为基础，旋律流畅而易于记忆，歌词（由亨利·帕科尔撰写）直白地表达了爱的渴望：「我爱你，我的生命和爱只为你……」这与萨蒂惯常的反讽和疏离态度截然不同——在这首歌里，他放下了所有的智识防线，写出了一首真实的情歌。

这首歌在萨蒂生前就已经颇受欢迎，也是他为数不多的能带来稳定版税收入的作品。时至今日，它以钢琴独奏版的形式被广泛演奏，是钢琴学习者和听众了解萨蒂另一面（温柔和感伤）的重要窗口，也是法国Belle Époque时代咖啡馆和音乐厅文化的完美音乐纪念。
famousScores.detailEn:
Je te veux (c. 1900) is a ballroom waltz Satie wrote for the chanteuse Paulette Darty, with whom he was enamored, and is the most immediately accessible of all his works. At that period Satie was earning his living playing in Montmartre bars and music halls, immersed in the tradition of French light music and chanson; this song perfectly inherits that tradition's elegance and melancholy.

Built on a simple but irresistible waltz rhythm with a flowing, memorable melody, the lyrics (by Henry Pacory) express the longing of love with directness: "Je te veux, ma vie et mon amour ne sont que pour toi…" This is entirely at odds with Satie's habitual irony and detachment — in this song he lowered all his intellectual defenses and wrote a genuine love song.

The piece was among the few to bring Satie reliable royalties during his lifetime. Today, in its piano solo arrangement, it is widely performed and serves as an important window into Satie's gentler, more sentimental side — as well as a perfect musical souvenir of Parisian café and music-hall culture in the Belle Époque.

famousScores.name: 《萨拉班德》三首（1887）
famousScores.desc: 萨蒂最早的重要作品，未解决和弦的先驱性运用令德彪西惊叹
famousScores.descEn: Satie's earliest important works — their unresolved chords astonished Debussy and prefigured much of Impressionism
famousScores.detail:
《萨拉班德》三首（1887）是萨蒂21岁时的作品，也是整个印象主义运动中最具先驱意义的早期作品之一。在这三首简短的钢琴曲中，萨蒂使用了大量未解决的九和弦平行进行——这在1887年是完全违反和声教科书的写法，但正是这种不解决的悬浮感，后来成为了德彪西和整个印象主义音乐的核心美学元素。

德彪西第一次听到这些作品时，据说被深深震撼，称之为「开创性的」。这两人此后成为了亲密的艺术伙伴（虽然关系时好时坏）。德彪西在自己的早期作品中借鉴了这种未解决和弦的平行进行手法，并将其发展成了更为复杂的印象主义和声语言。因此，萨蒂的《萨拉班德》可以说是德彪西整个印象主义风格的远端起源之一。

这三首作品今天常被用来说明一个历史论点：萨蒂在某种意义上比德彪西更早发明了「印象主义和声」，只是他没有将其发展为完整的音乐体系。他的这些早期作品以一种未加雕琢的原始状态，保存了一种音乐直觉的纯粹性，而这种纯粹性在德彪西手中被打磨为了精致的艺术。
famousScores.detailEn:
The three Sarabandes (1887) were composed when Satie was twenty-one and stand among the most historically significant early works in the entire Impressionist movement. In these brief piano pieces, Satie employs extensive parallel progressions of unresolved ninth chords — a practice that in 1887 flatly violated every harmony textbook, yet this very quality of unresolved suspension would go on to become a core aesthetic element of Debussy and the whole Impressionist idiom.

When Debussy first heard these pieces he was deeply struck, reportedly calling them "epoch-making." The two men became close artistic companions (though the relationship had its difficult periods). In his own early works, Debussy drew directly on this technique of parallel unresolved chords and developed it into the more elaborate Impressionist harmonic language. In this sense, Satie's Sarabandes can be regarded as one of the distant origins of Debussy's entire Impressionist style.

These three works are frequently cited today to make a historical argument: that in some sense Satie "invented" Impressionist harmony before Debussy did — he simply never developed it into a complete musical system. In their unpolished, raw state, they preserve a purity of musical instinct that Debussy would go on to refine into a high art.

influence: 萨蒂对20世纪音乐的影响是隐秘而深远的。德彪西从他早期的和弦平行进行中获得灵感；拉威尔称他为「先驱」。法国六人团以他为精神领袖，反对印象主义的朦胧。约翰·凯奇将他视为最重要的先驱——凯奇1963年组织了《烦恼》的首次完整演出。菲利普·格拉斯、史蒂夫·赖希等简约主义者承认萨蒂的开创之功。布莱恩·伊诺的环境音乐直接来源于「家具音乐」的理念。
influenceEn: Satie's influence on twentieth-century music is subtle yet far-reaching. Debussy drew inspiration from his early parallel chord voicings; Ravel called him a forerunner. Les Six adopted him as their spiritual leader in their revolt against Impressionist vagueness. John Cage regarded him as the most important precursor — Cage organized the first complete performance of Vexations in 1963. Minimalists such as Philip Glass and Steve Reich have acknowledged Satie's pioneering role. Brian Eno's ambient music traces directly back to the idea of furniture music.
